title: Citigroup (C) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: We ran a full discounted cash flow on Citigroup using FY2025 actuals. One number. Every assumption disclosed. Here's what Citigroup is really worth. Economic Prospect Score: 57/100 — Moderate Prospect.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-cwwxwlne>
Valuing banks requires estimating a Cost of Equity. With the 10Y Treasury at 4.18% and considering Citigroup's historical volatility and operational risks (beta ~1.4), a 10% discount rate provides a sufficient margin of safety and aligns with typical institutional return requirements for financial sector equities.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-cwwxwlne>
Citigroup has struggled with consistent earnings growth historically but is undergoing a massive restructuring under CEO Jane Fraser to shed less profitable consumer businesses and focus on wealth management and institutional clients. A 4% growth rate assumes these turnaround efforts yield modest success without heroic leaps in profitability.

verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).

faq.question: Why use Net Income instead of Free Cash Flow for a bank?
faq.answer: Westmount Research. "Citigroup (C) Intrinsic Value: A DCF Analysis." westmountfundamentals.com, March 18, 2026.

prospectVerdictDetail: Citigroup's turnaround efforts under Jane Fraser are yielding some results, particularly in streamlining operations and focusing on its core Institutional Clients Group (ICG). However, the bank still lags peers in return on tangible common equity (ROTCE) and faces intense competition. The ongoing transformation presents a moderate prospect, heavily reliant on flawless execution to close the valuation gap with its bulge bracket peers.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: Citigroup's momentum is stabilizing as it sheds non-core consumer markets, but revenue growth remains sluggish compared to top-tier competitors.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: Citi struggles to match the robust revenue growth of peers like JPMorgan or Morgan Stanley, though its Treasury and Trade Solutions (TTS) remains a bright spot with steady fee generation.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: The bank is intentionally shrinking its global consumer footprint, leading to near-term market share losses in retail, while trying to defend its share in investment banking and wealth management.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: In corporate banking and TTS, Citi has solid pricing power due to deep client integration. In consumer banking, it faces commoditization and fierce competition.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: Innovation is largely focused on digitizing existing wholesale banking services and modernizing legacy infrastructure rather than launching groundbreaking new products.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: The bank possesses a narrow moat stemming from switching costs in its massive corporate treasury operations and its status as a systemically important financial institution.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: Multinational corporations deeply embedded in Citi's Treasury and Trade Solutions face extremely high switching costs to migrate their complex global cash management to another provider.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: Network effects are present in its global payment clearing and institutional trading networks, though less pronounced than in consumer-facing payment networks.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: As a G-SIB (Global Systemically Important Bank), Citi benefits from an implicit 'too big to fail' backstop, but also endures the highest levels of regulatory scrutiny and capital requirements.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: Banking is inherently capital intensive. Citi's historical struggles with risk management have previously forced it to hold higher capital buffers, dragging down overall capital efficiency.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Sentiment is cautiously optimistic regarding the restructuring, but patience is wearing thin for tangible improvements in profitability metrics.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Revisions have been mixed, balancing the positive impact of cost-cutting and potential AI efficiencies against concerns over commercial real estate exposure and choppy investment banking fees.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The narrative is dominated by CEO Jane Fraser's sweeping reorganization and massive investments in AI infrastructure, presenting a 'show me' story for investors.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Management is aggressively shrinking the firm to grow returns and restarting share buybacks at depressed valuations, which is highly accretive if the turnaround succeeds.

prospectKeyRisks: Failure to execute the complex, multi-year reorganization could lead to further lagging returns and activist investor pressure.
Significant exposure to a global macroeconomic downturn or commercial real estate defaults could spike credit costs.
Persistent regulatory issues or data management consent orders could force unexpected capital retention or restrict growth.
prospectKeyCatalysts: Successful simplification driving ROTCE to 11-12% by 2027 would narrow the valuation gap with peers and trigger a re-rating from 0.6x to 0.8-1.0x tangible book value — a 30-60% upside opportunity

TTS and Security Services growth: Citi's institutional transaction banking is the crown jewel, growing 10%+ with high returns. If Citi can center its narrative around these businesses, the market may re-rate it as a payments/services company
Resolution of the Fed consent order would unlock meaningful share buyback capacity, allowing Citi to reduce its share count and drive EPS growth even with moderate revenue growth
prospectMethodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored C at 68/100 and Opus at 42/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
